key_insight: A deep learning model using screening breast ultrasound plus age and breast density can estimate a woman's 5-year breast cancer risk and separate higher- from lower-risk groups better than comparator risk models.
clinical_relevance: If validated externally, this approach could help personalize breast cancer risk assessment during routine ultrasound screening beyond density alone.
method: Retrospective study of 295,298 breast ultrasound exams from 122,072 women (2012–2020).
BUS-Risk-Net used attention-based multiple instance learning to aggregate image features and combined them with age and ultrasound-estimated breast density.
Performance was tested against Tyrer-Cuzick comparators and risk stratification was assessed within BI-RADS density groups.
evidence_signal: In the held-out screening test set, BUS-Risk-Net achieved an AUC of 0.679 for 5-year risk, outperforming the reduced Tyrer-Cuzick model (AUC 0.594); similar benefit was seen in a matched case-control cohort.
watch_item: The model is retrospective and single-system tested, so external and prospective validation are needed before clinical implementation.
paper_title: Five-Year Breast Cancer Risk Prediction From Screening Breast Ultrasound Using Deep Learning
